title: Cambridge man killed in early-morning shooting, investigators say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Xavier Bautista, 32, was found dead from a gunshot wound near Broadway and Norfolk Street in Cambridge.
The shooting occurred shortly before 4:30 a.m. and was reported by a pedestrian around 5:30 a.m.
Cambridge police and Massachusetts State Police are investigating; public urged to share any information via tips.

title: New England Aquarium trainer joins effort to save endangered penguin chicks in South Africa
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Diana Major from New England Aquarium volunteered in Cape Town with SANCCOB to save endangered penguin chicks.
African penguins are critically endangered, having lost 97% of their population, with possible extinction by 2035.
New England Aquarium supports African penguin conservation through staff volunteerism, funding, and public education initiatives.

title: Red Sox’ Willson Contreras apologizes for recent on-field confrontations: ‘It’s been a really tough week’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras is appealing a seven-game MLB suspension after a fight with Nationals' Cade Cavalli.
Venezuela's recent earthquakes deeply affected Contreras, contributing to his emotional state on and off the field.
Contreras apologized for recent on-field incidents, expressing hopes to prove himself through future actions.

title: Red Sox Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected to MLB All-Star Game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez will represent the Boston Red Sox in the 2026 MLB All-Star Game.
Suarez has a 2.94 ERA and 92 strikeouts in 88.2 innings this season, his first with the Red Sox.
Chapman set the record for most career strikeouts among relievers, with a 2.10 ERA and 17 saves in 2026.

title: Paul Pierce encourages Jaylen Brown after trade from Celtics: ‘This is what keeps you hungry’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Paul Pierce expressed disappointment but not surprise over Jaylen Brown's trade from the Celtics to the 76ers.
Pierce encouraged Brown to use the perceived disrespect as motivation to enhance his basketball career.
Brown criticized Brad Stevens and Boston's handling of trade discussions, citing a lack of respect.

title: Photos: Boston celebrates the Fourth of July
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Boston celebrated the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence with a parade and public reading at the Old State House.
Thousands gathered for the Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular, featuring performances by Lainey Wilson and Chance The Rapper.
Festivities included a drone show and fireworks display at the Hatch Memorial Shell, viewed from various locations in the city.

title: Is Caleb Durbin’s turnaround enough to solidify his future with the Red Sox?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Caleb Durbin leads the Red Sox in OPS over 30 days, showing significant improvement from early 2026 struggles.
Durbin's recent performance includes seven home runs, 14 RBIs, and a .321 average since May 28.
The Red Sox have team control over Durbin until 2031, providing roster flexibility with his current deal.

title: Red Sox’ Aroldis Chapman breaks MLB relief pitcher strikeout record in save vs. Angels
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Aroldis Chapman surpassed Hoyt Wilhelm's record with his 1,364th strikeout, becoming MLB's top relief pitcher in strikeouts.
Chapman secured the Red Sox's 5-2 win over the Los Angeles Angels, striking out Denzer Guzman during the game.
Chapman, a two-time World Series champion, achieved the milestone over 847 innings compared to Wilhelm's 1,872.1 innings.

title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston ranks as the most collision-prone city in the U.S., averaging 3.7 years between crashes.
Worcester and Springfield, MA, also ranked among the worst, placing 197th and 196th respectively.
Brownsville, Texas leads as the safest city for drivers, averaging nearly 15 years between collisions.

title: Falmouth man, 74, arrested after police say he led troopers on wrong-way, high-speed chase over Bourne Bridge
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Armando Deleon, 74, from Falmouth, led police on a high-speed, wrong-way chase over the Bourne Bridge.
Deleon was arraigned on more than a dozen charges, including assault and battery on a police officer and drug possession.
A judge held Deleon without bail; he is scheduled for a pretrial hearing on August 5 in Falmouth District Court.
